mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, forcing equity repricing downward over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that supports corporate margins, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 58% of observations during tightening periods, or the signal predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate creates a negative real rate environment that compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.4% while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: The interaction yields positive 20-day returns in > 62% of triggered months, or the combined signal shows no incremental predictive value over CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains aggregate income and consumer spending, providing a structural floor for corporate earnings and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of months with positive payroll growth, or the correlation between payroll changes and quarterly returns falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate risk-off positioning as recession fears materialize, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of contraction quarters, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a liquidity expansion signals weak demand transmission that undermines equity valuations, producing negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances during liquidity expansions, or the signal accuracy drops below 45% in out-of-sample periods.
